signals.reasoning: BTC remains firm, but the edge is still too mild to justify a fresh direction under a caution regime.
ETH is still the cleanest reduced-size long because price and open interest are rising together while funding remains flat.
SOL still reads as a short-covering rally rather than a durable continuation entry, so I would keep it watch-only.
BLAST still qualifies as a reduced-size squeeze long because funding is extremely negative while price is up, but poor liquidity keeps conviction capped.
GRIFFAIN is already in a flush state and too low-liquidity to trust for a serious fresh trade.
SPX is the best secondary squeeze long on this board because funding remains clearly negative while price and open interest are both building in its favor.
HEMI remains a valid reduced-size fade because price is rising on a short-covering pattern while funding is already positive and crowding is elevated.
VVV still has enough squeeze fuel and trend support to avoid shorting, but it is already too extended to chase confidently here.
TON remains the clearest reduced-size peak fade because it is still very extended and funding has turned modestly positive.
